description: Final polish: enhance diagrams, add coverage badge, improve README experiment framing, and add supplementary visualizations to clearly communicate the project as an AI-driven TDD experiment
steps: Step 1 - Add a coverage badge to README.md. Since CI already generates coverage.out artifacts (see .github/workflows/ci.yml), add a step in CI to compute the total coverage percentage and generate a badge. Use a simple approach: add a 'Go Coverage' badge after the existing badges. The badge can use shields.io with a static label initially (e.g. 93%+ based on the known Lambda coverage from FINAL-REPORT.md), or better, add a CI step that uses 'go tool cover -func' to extract the percentage and uploads a dynamic badge via a gist or shields.io endpoint. For simplicity and reliability in this final polish, add a static shields.io badge showing '93%+' coverage (from FINAL-REPORT.md metrics) in the badge row, linking to the CI workflow.

Step 2 - Add supplementary Mermaid diagrams to ARCHITECTURE.md: (a) Add a 'Development Workflow' diagram showing the TDD cycle as a flowchart (Red -> Green -> Refactor -> Repeat), (b) Add an 'Issue Progression Timeline' diagram using a Mermaid gantt chart showing how the 14 issues progressed over the 16-day development period (data from EXPERIMENT.md issue history table), (c) Add a 'Test Layer Coverage' diagram showing the 7 test layers as a hierarchy/mindmap.

Step 3 - Update README.md to enhance the experiment framing: (a) Add a prominent callout/blockquote near the top (after the first paragraph) that clearly positions this as an experiment and invites readers to draw their own conclusions, (b) Add a 'Self-Grading' subsection under 'Experiment Methodology' that summarizes the scores from FINAL-REPORT.md (Goal scores table) and links to the full report, (c) Add a 'Draw Your Own Conclusions' subsection that frames the project as one data point in a broader matrix and invites critical evaluation, (d) Add a 'Metrics at a Glance' table (from FINAL-REPORT.md key metrics) in the experiment section for quick visual reference.

Step 4 - Add a 'Visualizations' section to README.md (before 'Project Structure') that shows a condensed version of the development timeline and links to ARCHITECTURE.md for full diagrams. Include a small inline Mermaid diagram showing the experiment matrix (5 languages x 3 AIs) with the Go+Kiro cell highlighted.

Step 5 - Minor visual/workflow improvements: (a) Add a 'Tests' badge showing the number of test functions (80) using shields.io static badge, (b) Add an 'Issues' badge showing 14/14 closed, (c) Ensure all README sections have consistent formatting and that the Table of Contents is updated to include any new sections.
acceptance_criteria: README.md has at least 6 badges in the header (CI, Go, AWS CDK, License, Coverage, Tests)
ARCHITECTURE.md has at least 3 Mermaid diagrams (existing system diagram + current state diagram + at least one new supplementary diagram)
README.md contains a prominent experiment callout/blockquote near the top inviting readers to evaluate
README.md has a 'Self-Grading' subsection with the goal scores from FINAL-REPORT.md
README.md has a section that frames this as one cell in a 5x3 experiment matrix
All Mermaid diagrams use valid Mermaid syntax (flowchart, gantt, or mindmap types)
README Table of Contents is updated to include all new sections
No infrastructure code is modified (this is docs-only)
verification: NODE_OPTIONS='' go test -v -count=1 ./... (tests still pass - no code changes)
Visually confirm README.md has 6+ badges in header
Grep for 'mermaid' in ARCHITECTURE.md to confirm 3+ diagram blocks exist
Grep for 'Self-Grading' or 'self-grading' in README.md to confirm section exists
Grep for 'Draw Your Own Conclusions' in README.md to confirm section exists
